159	// scriptHarnessExecutor builds an ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc that:

162	//     calls Land() — serialized via landMutexFor(projectRoot).
163	//
164	// Both ExecuteBead and Land() are serialized per projectRoot. This is required
165	// because ExecuteBead calls CommitTracker (which acquires the git index lock)
166	// and because Land() uses CAS-based UpdateRefTo. In production, the server
167	// dispatches one bead per worker and serializes Land() via the LandCoordinator
168	// goroutine; in integration tests, the same mutex handles both.

commit c650005cb9b43c18251e72f0c62f4d54f508f5f7
Author: Erik LaBianca <erik.labianca@gmail.com>
Date:   Fri May 8 21:21:20 2026 -0400

    fix(agent): retry git read-tree HEAD on index lock contention [ddx-7e659c95]
    
    After a merge landing, UpdateRefTo advances the target branch ref without
    updating the main worktree index, leaving staged phantom reverts of the
    bead's changes. syncWorkTreeToHeadGuarded fixes this via git read-tree HEAD,
    but silently discarded the error when .git/index.lock was held by a
    concurrent operator git process (git status, git commit, etc.). When an
    operator subsequently ran `git commit beads.jsonl`, the phantom staged
    entries (reverts of evidence files, modifications of bead-changed files)
    were swept in, undoing the bead's work.
    
    Add readTreeHeadWithRetry which loops with exponential backoff (100ms -> 1s,
    30s budget) when git output contains "index.lock". Add regression test
    TestLand_MergeRequired_IndexCleanAfterMerge that holds .git/index.lock
    for 2s during Land() and asserts the main worktree index is clean after
    Land() returns.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

#!/bin/bash
file="internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go"

# Get list of test function lines that don't have t.Parallel() in the next few lines
while IFS= read -r line; do
  linenum=$(echo "$line" | cut -d: -f1)
  # Check if t.Parallel() exists in lines after this function
  has_parallel=$(sed -n "${linenum},$((linenum+3))p" "$file" | grep -c "t.Parallel()")
  
  if [ "$has_parallel" -eq 0 ]; then
    # Find the first line that starts with a tab or space (the function body)
    body_line=$((linenum+1))
    first_stmt=$(sed -n "${body_line}p" "$file")
    
    # If it's not already t.Parallel(), we need to add it
    if ! echo "$first_stmt" | grep -q "t.Parallel()"; then
      echo "$linenum: $first_stmt"
    fi
  fi
done < <(grep -n "^func Test" "$file")
EOF

119				return IndexLockRecoveryResult{Reason: "lock not present"}, nil
120			}
121			return IndexLockRecoveryResult{}, fmt.Errorf("stat %s: %w", lockPath, statErr)
122		}
123		age := time.Since(info.ModTime())
124		pid, _ := IndexLockOwner(lockPath)
125	
126		if pid > 0 {
127			alive := processAlive(pid)
128			if !alive {
129				if rmErr := os.Remove(lockPath); rmErr != nil && !os.IsNotExist(rmErr) {
130					return IndexLockRecoveryResult{OwnerPID: pid, Age: age},
131						fmt.Errorf("remove lock owned by dead pid %d: %w", pid, rmErr)
132				}
133				return IndexLockRecoveryResult{
134					Removed: true, OwnerPID: pid, OwnerAlive: false, Age: age,
135					Reason: fmt.Sprintf("removed: owner pid %d not alive (lock age %s)", pid, age.Round(time.Second)),
136				}, nil
137			}
138			return IndexLockRecoveryResult{
139				OwnerPID: pid, OwnerAlive: true, Age: age,
140				Reason: fmt.Sprintf("live owner pid %d (lock age %s)", pid, age.Round(time.Second)),
141			}, nil
142		}
143	
144		if age >= StaleAge {
145			if rmErr := os.Remove(lockPath); rmErr != nil && !os.IsNotExist(rmErr) {
146				return IndexLockRecoveryResult{Age: age},
147					fmt.Errorf("remove unowned stale lock (age %s): %w", age, rmErr)
148			}
149			return IndexLockRecoveryResult{
150				Removed: true, Age: age,
151				Reason: fmt.Sprintf("removed: no owner found, age %s >= stale threshold %s",
152					age.Round(time.Second), StaleAge),
153			}, nil
154		}
155	
156		return IndexLockRecoveryResult{
157			Age:    age,
158			Reason: fmt.Sprintf("no owner found but lock is fresh (age %s < %s)", age.Round(time.Second), StaleAge),
159		}, nil
160	}
161	
162	// RunGitWithIndexLockRecovery runs `git args...` in dir and, on
163	// .git/index.lock contention, identifies the owner and either removes a stale
164	// lock or briefly waits for a live owner before retrying.
165	//
166	// Total attempts are bounded by RecoveryAttempts; the final error is decorated
167	// with the most recent recovery diagnostic. The helper deliberately does NOT
168	// loop for tens of seconds — stalling is not acceptable here.
169	func RunGitWithIndexLockRecovery(ctx context.Context, dir string, args ...string) ([]byte, error) {
170		var out []byte
171		err := lockmetrics.Instrument("index.lock", indexLockOperation(args), func() error {
172			var e error
173			out, e = runGitWithIndexLockRecovery(ctx, dir, args...)
174			return e
175		})
176		return out, err
177	}
178	
179	// indexLockOperation labels an index.lock hold by the git subcommand that
180	// took it, e.g. "index.commit" or "index.add".
181	func indexLockOperation(args []string) string {
182		if len(args) == 0 {
183			return "index"
184		}
185		return "index." + args[0]
186	}
187	
188	func runGitWithIndexLockRecovery(ctx context.Context, dir string, args ...string) ([]byte, error) {
189		var lastOut []byte
190		var lastErr error
191		var lastDiag string
192		for attempt := 0; attempt < RecoveryAttempts; attempt++ {
193			out, err := internalgit.Command(ctx, dir, args...).CombinedOutput()
194			if err == nil {
195				return out, nil
196			}
197			if !IsIndexLockError(string(out)) {
198				return out, err
199			}
200			lastOut = out
201			lastErr = err
202			result, recErr := RecoverGitIndexLock(dir)
203			if recErr != nil {
204				return out, fmt.Errorf("%s; index-lock recovery failed: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), recErr)
205			}
206			lastDiag = result.Reason
207			if !result.Removed {
208				time.Sleep(LiveOwnerWait)
209			}
210		}
211		if lastDiag == "" {
212			return lastOut, lastErr
213		}
214		return lastOut, fmt.Errorf("%s; index-lock owner: %s: %w",
215			strings.TrimSpace(string(lastOut)), lastDiag, lastErr)
216	}
